summary: High‑energy 1970 Port Chester show featuring a stellar Other One → Dark Star → St. Stephen sequence captured on surprisingly clear audience tape.
review: Fans rave about this non‑soundboard recording, saying it sounds better than many later board tapes and is a joy to listen to with a good EQ and headphones. The performance shines especially during the Other One > Dark Star > St. Stephen stretch, which reviewers single out as the highlight of the night.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are highly positive (4–5 stars), praising both the unexpected recording clarity and the standout second‑set sequence; weighting of show quality (70%) and recording quality (30%) yields a rating in the high‑four range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Not a soundboard tape, but listeners describe it as clearer than many later board recordings; recommend EQ and good headphones for optimal experience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Port Chester shows are regarded as a class of their own; the Other One → Dark Star → St. Stephen sequence is highlighted as the peak, indicating strong pacing and flow.
